title: Citrusy Creamy Filipino Chicken Curry
description: A vibrant Filipino-inspired chicken curry that balances creamy coconut, zesty citrus, and fragrant massaman spices, perfect for a quick solo dinner.
instructions: In a large pot, heat coconut oil over medium heat. Add diced onion, minced garlic, and minced ginger. Sauté until fragrant, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the massaman curry paste and cook for 1 minute to release its flavors.
Add the chicken pieces, coating them with the paste. Cook until the chicken begins to brown, about 5 minutes.
Pour in the coconut milk, then add sliced carrot, cubed potato, and sliced bell pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, reduce heat to low, cover, and let it cook for 15 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
Season with salt, pepper, lime juice, and orange juice. Stir everything together.
Transfer the curry into an oven‑safe dish and broil on high for 4–5 minutes, watching closely, until the top is slightly caramelized and the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 165 °F (74 °C).
Remove from the oven, let it rest for 5 minutes, then serve hot. (Optional: serve with cauliflower rice.)
